title: Signal skips flip-flop under some circumstances
body:
Hi there!

I encountered a surprising issue where the Verilator (`Verilator 5.003 devel rev v4.228-192-g79682e607`) output does not comply with the SystemVerilog input.
[To help you reproduce the issue, I created a repository here](https://github.com/flaviens/veriskew).
In short, according to the snippet below, the signal `signal_1154` should follow `signal_0672`, but under some circumstances, they both take the same value simultaneously.

```
  always_ff @(posedge clk_i, negedge rst_ni)
    if (!rst_ni) signal_1154 <= 64'h0000000000000000;
    else signal_1154 <= signal_0672;
```

I analyzed a bit the C++ code generated by Verilator and it seems that the cause is a wrong evaluation order during the clock positive edge, where first `signal_0672` takes its new value, and this new value propagates directly to `signal_1154` during the same `eval()` call, but my analysis may be erroneous.

Additionally, the flip-flop skipping does not always happen. In the repo provided above for example, at tick 52, the signal `signal_1154` correctly takes the value of `signal_0672` with one cycle of latency.

If you have any question please feel free to ask!

Thank you for your help!
Flavien
